# Test Template Output Examples

These are examples of what the test templates will generate when consumed by LzyCrud.

## Example 1: Domain Entity Test (Tests.Domain.tt)

**Input**: Entity `Product` in `ProductAgg` aggregate

**Generated Output**: `ProductAgg.ProductEntityTests.g.cs`

```csharp
// <auto-generated>
//     This code was generated by a T4 template (Tests.Domain.tt).
//     Changes to this file will be lost when the code is regenerated.
//     DO NOT EDIT this file manually.
// </auto-generated>

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated by LazyCrud. DO NOT EDIT.
/// Generated for: Product in Domain layer.
/// </summary>

using Xunit;
using MyCompany.Product.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Entities;

namespace MyCompany.Product.Test.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Tests;

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated tests for entity Product.
/// </summary>
public class ProductEntityTests
{
    [Fact]
    public void Product_Should_Be_Instantiable()
    {
        // Arrange & Act
        var entity = new Product();

        // Assert
        Assert.NotNull(entity);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void Product_Should_Have_Default_Id()
    {
        // Arrange & Act
        var entity = new Product();

        // Assert — Entity base class sets Id
        Assert.True(entity.Id == default || entity.Id != default,
            "Entity Id should be initialized by the base class.");
    }

    [Fact]
    public void Product_ToString_Should_Not_Be_Empty()
    {
        // Arrange
        var entity = new Product();

        // Act
        var result = entity.ToString();

        // Assert
        Assert.False(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(result),
            "Product.ToString() should not return empty.");
    }

    [Fact]
    public void Product_Name_Should_Accept_Value()
    {
        // Arrange
        var entity = new Product();
        var expected = "test-value";

        // Act
        entity.Name = expected;

        // Assert
        Assert.Equal(expected, entity.Name);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void Product_Description_Should_Accept_Value()
    {
        // Arrange
        var entity = new Product();
        var expected = "test-value";

        // Act
        entity.Description = expected;

        // Assert
        Assert.Equal(expected, entity.Description);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void Product_Price_Should_Have_Default_Value()
    {
        // Arrange
        var entity = new Product();

        // Act
        var value = entity.Price;

        // Assert
        Assert.Equal(default(decimal), value);
    }
}
```

---

## Example 2: DTO Test (Tests.DTO.tt)

**Input**: DTO for `Product` entity

**Generated Output**: `ProductAgg.ProductDtoTests.g.cs`

```csharp
// <auto-generated>
//     This code was generated by a T4 template (Tests.Application.DTO.tt).
//     Changes to this file will be lost when the code is regenerated.
//     DO NOT EDIT this file manually.
// </auto-generated>

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated by LazyCrud. DO NOT EDIT.
/// Generated for: Product in Application.DTO layer.
/// </summary>

using System.Text.Json;
using Xunit;

namespace MyCompany.Product.Test.Application.DTO.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Tests;

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated tests for DTO ProductDTO.
/// </summary>
public class ProductDtoTests
{
    [Fact]
    public void ProductDTO_Should_Be_Instantiable()
    {
        // Arrange & Act
        var dto = new MyCompany.Product.Test.Application.DTO.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Requests.ProductDTO();

        // Assert
        Assert.NotNull(dto);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductDTO_Should_Be_Serializable()
    {
        // Arrange
        var dto = new MyCompany.Product.Test.Application.DTO.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Requests.ProductDTO();

        // Act
        var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(dto);
        var deserialized = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<MyCompany.Product.Test.Application.DTO.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Requests.ProductDTO>(json);

        // Assert
        Assert.NotNull(json);
        Assert.NotNull(deserialized);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductDTO_Should_RoundTrip_Serialize()
    {
        // Arrange
        var dto = new MyCompany.Product.Test.Application.DTO.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Requests.ProductDTO();
        dto.Name = "sample-name";
        dto.Description = "sample-description";

        // Act
        var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(dto);
        var deserialized = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<MyCompany.Product.Test.Application.DTO.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Requests.ProductDTO>(json);

        // Assert
        Assert.NotNull(deserialized);
        Assert.Equal(dto.Name, deserialized.Name);
        Assert.Equal(dto.Description, deserialized.Description);
    }
}
```

---

## Example 3: API Controller Test (Tests.Api.tt)

**Input**: `Product` entity with `[EndpointsT4("All")]` attribute

**Generated Output**: `ProductAgg.ProductControllerTests.g.cs`

```csharp
// <auto-generated>
//     This code was generated by a T4 template (Tests.Api.tt).
//     Changes to this file will be lost when the code is regenerated.
//     DO NOT EDIT this file manually.
// </auto-generated>

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated by LazyCrud. DO NOT EDIT.
/// Generated for: Product in Api layer.
/// </summary>

using Xunit;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;

namespace MyCompany.Product.Test.Api.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Tests;

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated tests for API layer — Product controller.
/// Validates controller structure and endpoint availability (smoke tests).
/// </summary>
public class ProductControllerTests
{
    [Fact]
    public void ProductController_Should_Exist()
    {
        // Validates that the controller type is discoverable at compile time.
        var type = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Api.Controllers.ProductController);
        Assert.NotNull(type);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductController_Should_Inherit_ControllerBase()
    {
        // Arrange
        var type = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Api.Controllers.ProductController);

        // Assert
        Assert.True(type.IsSubclassOf(typeof(ControllerBase)) || type.IsSubclassOf(typeof(Controller)),
            $"ProductController should inherit from ControllerBase or Controller.");
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductController_Should_Have_Route_Attribute()
    {
        // Arrange
        var type = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Api.Controllers.ProductController);

        // Act
        var routeAttr = type.GetCustomAttributes(typeof(RouteAttribute), false).FirstOrDefault();

        // Assert
        Assert.NotNull(routeAttr);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductController_Should_Have_Action_Methods()
    {
        // Arrange
        var type = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Api.Controllers.ProductController);

        // Act
        var actionMethods = type.GetMethods()
            .Where(m => m.IsPublic && !m.IsSpecialName)
            .ToList();

        // Assert
        Assert.True(actionMethods.Count > 0,
            $"ProductController should have at least one public action method.");
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductController_Should_Have_Post_Method()
    {
        // Arrange
        var type = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Api.Controllers.ProductController);

        // Act
        var postMethods = type.GetMethods()
            .Where(m => m.GetCustomAttributes(typeof(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.HttpPostAttribute), false).Any())
            .ToList();

        // Assert
        Assert.NotEmpty(postMethods);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductController_Should_Have_Get_Method()
    {
        // Arrange
        var type = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Api.Controllers.ProductController);

        // Act
        var getMethods = type.GetMethods()
            .Where(m => m.GetCustomAttributes(typeof(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.HttpGetAttribute), false).Any())
            .ToList();

        // Assert
        Assert.NotEmpty(getMethods);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductController_Should_Have_Put_Method()
    {
        // Arrange
        var type = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Api.Controllers.ProductController);

        // Act
        var putMethods = type.GetMethods()
            .Where(m => m.GetCustomAttributes(typeof(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.HttpPutAttribute), false).Any())
            .ToList();

        // Assert
        Assert.NotEmpty(putMethods);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductController_Should_Have_Delete_Method()
    {
        // Arrange
        var type = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Api.Controllers.ProductController);

        // Act
        var deleteMethods = type.GetMethods()
            .Where(m => m.GetCustomAttributes(typeof(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.HttpDeleteAttribute), false).Any())
            .ToList();

        // Assert
        Assert.NotEmpty(deleteMethods);
    }
}
```

---

## Example 4: IoC Tests (Tests.InfraIoC.tt)

**Input**: `ProductAgg` aggregate with multiple entities

**Generated Output**: `ProductAgg.IoCTests.g.cs`

```csharp
// <auto-generated>
//     This code was generated by a T4 template (Tests.Infra.IoC.tt).
//     Changes to this file will be lost when the code is regenerated.
//     DO NOT EDIT this file manually.
// </auto-generated>

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated by LazyCrud. DO NOT EDIT.
/// Generated for: ProductAgg in Infra.IoC layer.
/// </summary>

using Xunit;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;

namespace MyCompany.Product.Test.Infra.IoC.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Tests;

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated tests for Infra.IoC layer — ProductAgg aggregate.
/// Validates dependency injection configuration and service registration.
/// </summary>
public class ProductAggIoCTests
{
    [Fact]
    public void IoCFactory_Should_Register_Services_For_ProductAgg()
    {
        // Arrange
        var services = new ServiceCollection();

        // Act — Attempt to resolve IoC configuration type
        var iocFactoryType = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Infra.IoC.IoCFactory);

        // Assert
        Assert.NotNull(iocFactoryType);
        Assert.True(iocFactoryType.IsClass, "IoCFactory should be a class.");
    }

    [Fact]
    public void IoCFactory_Should_Have_RegisterDependencies_Method()
    {
        // Arrange
        var iocFactoryType = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Infra.IoC.IoCFactory);

        // Act
        var methods = iocFactoryType.GetMethods()
            .Where(m => m.Name.Contains("Register") || m.Name.Contains("Configure"))
            .ToList();

        // Assert
        Assert.NotEmpty(methods);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void Should_Resolve_Product_Repository_Type()
    {
        // Validates that Product repository interface is resolvable.
        var type = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Repositories.IProductRepository);
        
        Assert.NotNull(type);
        Assert.True(type.IsInterface, "Repository should be an interface.");
    }

    [Fact]
    public void Should_Resolve_Product_AppService_Type()
    {
        // Validates that Product application service interface is resolvable.
        var type = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Test.Application.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Interfaces.IProductAppService);
        
        Assert.NotNull(type);
        Assert.True(type.IsInterface, "AppService should be an interface.");
    }
}
```

---

## Example 5: QueryModel Test (Tests.ApiQueries.tt)

**Input**: `Product` entity with query filters

**Generated Output**: `ProductAgg.ProductQueryModelTests.g.cs`

```csharp
// <auto-generated>
//     This code was generated by a T4 template (Tests.Api.Queries.tt).
//     Changes to this file will be lost when the code is regenerated.
//     DO NOT EDIT this file manually.
// </auto-generated>

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated by LazyCrud. DO NOT EDIT.
/// Generated for: Product in Api.Queries layer.
/// </summary>

using Xunit;
using System.Text.Json;

namespace MyCompany.Product.Test.Api.Queries.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Tests;

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated tests for QueryModel — ProductQueryModel.
/// Validates query model structure and serialization.
/// </summary>
public class ProductQueryModelTests
{
    [Fact]
    public void ProductQueryModel_Should_Be_Instantiable()
    {
        // Arrange & Act
        var queryModel = new MyCompany.Product.Test.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Queries.Models.ProductQueryModel();

        // Assert
        Assert.NotNull(queryModel);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductQueryModel_Should_Have_Default_Pagination()
    {
        // Arrange & Act
        var queryModel = new MyCompany.Product.Test.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Queries.Models.ProductQueryModel();

        // Assert — QueryModel base should provide pagination properties
        Assert.NotNull(queryModel);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductQueryModel_Should_Be_Serializable()
    {
        // Arrange
        var queryModel = new MyCompany.Product.Test.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Queries.Models.ProductQueryModel();

        // Act
        var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(queryModel);
        var deserialized = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<MyCompany.Product.Test.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Queries.Models.ProductQueryModel>(json);

        // Assert
        Assert.NotNull(json);
        Assert.NotNull(deserialized);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductQueryModel_Name_Should_Filter()
    {
        // Arrange
        var queryModel = new MyCompany.Product.Test.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Queries.Models.ProductQueryModel();
        var testValue = "test-filter-name";

        // Act
        queryModel.Name = testValue;

        // Assert
        Assert.Equal(testValue, queryModel.Name);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductQueryModel_Should_Support_OrderBy()
    {
        // Arrange
        var queryModel = new MyCompany.Product.Test.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Queries.Models.ProductQueryModel();

        // Act
        queryModel.OrderBy = "Id";

        // Assert
        Assert.Equal("Id", queryModel.OrderBy);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductQueryModel_Should_Support_Pagination()
    {
        // Arrange
        var queryModel = new MyCompany.Product.Test.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Queries.Models.ProductQueryModel();

        // Act
        queryModel.Page = 2;
        queryModel.PageSize = 50;

        // Assert
        Assert.Equal(2, queryModel.Page);
        Assert.Equal(50, queryModel.PageSize);
    }
}
```

---

## Example 6: Enumeration Test (Tests.Enumerations.tt)

**Input**: Enum `ProductStatus` in `ProductAgg`

**Generated Output**: `ProductAgg.EnumerationsTests.g.cs`

```csharp
// <auto-generated>
//     This code was generated by a T4 template (Tests.Enumerations.tt).
//     Changes to this file will be lost when the code is regenerated.
//     DO NOT EDIT this file manually.
// </auto-generated>

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated by LazyCrud. DO NOT EDIT.
/// Generated for: ProductAgg in Enumerations layer.
/// </summary>

using Xunit;
using System;

namespace MyCompany.Product.Test.Enumerations.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Tests;

/// <summary>
/// Auto-generated tests for Enumerations in ProductAgg aggregate.
/// Validates enum parsing, values, and ToString behavior.
/// </summary>
public class ProductAggEnumerationsTests
{
    [Fact]
    public void ProductStatus_Should_Have_Members()
    {
        // Arrange
        var enumType = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Enums.ProductStatus);

        // Act
        var values = Enum.GetValues(enumType);

        // Assert
        Assert.NotEmpty(values);
        Assert.True(values.Length > 0, "ProductStatus should have at least one member.");
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductStatus_Should_Parse_From_String()
    {
        // Arrange
        var enumType = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Enums.ProductStatus);
        var firstValue = Enum.GetValues(enumType).GetValue(0);
        var stringValue = firstValue.ToString();

        // Act
        var parsed = Enum.Parse(enumType, stringValue);

        // Assert
        Assert.NotNull(parsed);
        Assert.Equal(firstValue, parsed);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductStatus_ToString_Should_Return_Name()
    {
        // Arrange
        var enumType = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Enums.ProductStatus);
        var firstValue = Enum.GetValues(enumType).GetValue(0);

        // Act
        var stringValue = firstValue.ToString();

        // Assert
        Assert.False(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(stringValue),
            "ProductStatus.ToString() should return the enum member name.");
    }

    [Theory]
    [InlineData("Active")]
    [InlineData("Inactive")]
    [InlineData("Discontinued")]
    public void ProductStatus_Should_Parse_Valid_Members(string memberName)
    {
        // Arrange
        var enumType = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Enums.ProductStatus);

        // Act
        var canParse = Enum.TryParse(enumType, memberName, out var result);

        // Assert
        Assert.True(canParse, $"Should be able to parse '{memberName}' as ProductStatus");
        Assert.NotNull(result);
    }

    [Fact]
    public void ProductStatus_Should_Reject_Invalid_Parse()
    {
        // Arrange
        var enumType = typeof(MyCompany.Product.Domain.Aggregates.ProductAgg.Enums.ProductStatus);
        var invalidValue = "Invalid_ProductStatus_Value_XYZ";

        // Act
        var canParse = Enum.TryParse(enumType, invalidValue, out _);

        // Assert
        Assert.False(canParse, $"Should not parse invalid value '{invalidValue}' as ProductStatus");
    }
}
```

---

## Key Characteristics of Generated Tests

1. **Auto-generated headers**: Clear indication these files should not be edited manually
2. **XML documentation**: Summary comments with "DO NOT EDIT" warning
3. **xUnit framework**: Uses `[Fact]` and `[Theory]` attributes
4. **Compile-time validation**: Tests validate types exist at compile time (no runtime dependencies needed for basic structure tests)
5. **Minimal but effective**: Each test validates one specific aspect
6. **Extensible**: Users can add their own test files alongside generated ones

## File Naming Convention

Generated test files follow this pattern:
- `{AggName}.{EntityName}{TestType}Tests.g.cs`
- Examples:
  - `ProductAgg.ProductEntityTests.g.cs`
  - `ProductAgg.ProductDtoTests.g.cs`
  - `ProductAgg.ProductControllerTests.g.cs`
  - `ProductAgg.IoCTests.g.cs`

The `.g.cs` extension clearly indicates these are generated files.
